data.title: Dow stays flat post Fed rate decision; Nvidia stock rises; Meta, Microsoft soar on earnings.
data.description: Following the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, the Dow Jones index remained unchanged. Nvidia's stock experienced a surge after a price-target increase. Today's stock market saw the Dow decline following comments from Fed Chairman Powell, while Meta and Microsoft both saw significant gains in their earnings reports.

data.title: Actelis Networks secures a significant Tier 2 carrier win, expands in UK telecom market with hybrid-fiber tech.
data.description: The recent purchase showcases the significance of Actelis' hybrid-fiber technology for carrier-grade enterprise uses, boosting the Company's presence in the UK telecommunications sector. This order emphasizes Actelis's innovative technology, underlining its relevance for enterprise applications in the UK telecommunications market.

data.title: Accumulus Synergy creates Accumulus Technologies to expand regulatory platform for global therapy access.
data.description: Accumulus Synergy has spun out a new entity called Accumulus Technologies with the goal of expanding its regulatory platform and speeding up the delivery of therapies to patients worldwide. This move is aimed at enhancing access to treatments for a larger number of individuals.

data.title: Trump imposes 25% tariff on Indian imports due to Russian oil trade, doubling total tariff to 50%.
data.description: President Donald Trump has increased tariffs on Indian imports by 25%, bringing the total tariff burden on Indian goods to 50%. This decision came in response to India's purchase of Russian oil, which the White House views as a threat to U.S. national security and foreign policy interests. The announcement was made through an executive order on August 6. The move is part of efforts to address the national emergency declared over Russian aggression and its economic support network. The INDA ETF is experiencing significant movement as a result. For further details, the full story is available on the website Benzinga.com.
Market making big moves, hitting record highs with the S&P 500 reaching 4,500 and the Nasdaq hitting 15,000.

data.title: LyondellBasell (NYSE: LYB) declares $1.37 per share dividend, payable on Sept. 2, 2025; ex-dividend and record date on Aug. 25, 2025.
data.description: On August 4, 2025, LyondellBasell declared a dividend of $1.37 per share for its shareholders. The payment date for this dividend is set for September 2, 2025, while the ex-dividend and record date is scheduled for August 25, 2025.

data.title: Docyt launches High Precision Accounting Intelligence (HpAI) for automation of complex accounting tasks with unmatched precision.
data.description: Docyt, based in Mountain View, California, has introduced High Precision Accounting Intelligence (HpAI), a specialized tool designed to automate intricate accounting processes with exceptional accuracy. This innovative technology aims to streamline accounting workflows with unparalleled precision.

data.title: Trump’s economic approval ratings mirroring those of Biden, starting to resemble predecessor.
data.description: The current economic approval ratings of President Donald Trump are beginning to mirror those of the former president.

data.title: Joinable Labs launches with $2M seed round and new product RAG in a BOX to accelerate Time-to-Intelligence for Private AI.
data.description: In San Francisco, Joinable Labs has recently made its official debut, revealing its aim to assist AI Builders in expediting Time-to-Intelligence (TTI), which denotes the time required to transition from disorganized data to a functional, efficient production Private AI system. The organization introduced its first product, RAG in a BOX, which enables AI Builders to prototype and implement AI systems with exclusive data at a significantly accelerated pace — potentially 50 times quicker compared to conventional methods.
Giga Metals finishes initial round of private funding, raising undisclosed amount.

data.title: Approov secures £5M Series A funding led by Maven Capital Partners to enhance mobile app security.
data.description: Approov Limited, a prominent player in mobile app and API security, has concluded a successful Series A funding round of £5 million (approximately US$6.7 million). The Investment Fund for Scotland, overseen by Maven Capital Partners, took the lead in this investment, together with contributions from Souter Investments and current investors Lanza techVentures and Scottish Enterprise. This funding injection will support Approov in enhancing its Research & Development efforts.
